prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a simplified model assuming a geometric Brownian motion for stock prices. For the point estimate, a slight positive daily drift (derived from an assumed 8% annual return) was applied over 20 trading days to the current price. For the confidence intervals, an assumed annualized volatility of 22% was used (as no specific historical volatility data was provided), which was then scaled to a 20-day period. The intervals are constructed around the point estimate using the estimated 20-day volatility and standard Z-scores for each confidence level, assuming a log-normal distribution for stock prices.
